mode: smoke_test (first AP Biology run — only M1 end-to-end)
scope_note: AP Calculus AB COMPLETE (12 modules). AP CS A COMPLETE (10 modules, M4-M9 awaiting Mac upload). AP Psychology COMPLETE (16 modules, mid-course awaiting Mac upload). Per AUTO_PIPELINE Step 1 subject-diversity rule (last 3+ runs were math), pivoted to AP Biology — Science. No CED reference doc existed for AP Bio; this run wrote references/ap-biology-m1-ref.md as a snapshotted M1-only reference (later expanded after Round 1 review feedback).
throughput_strategy: Sequential generator → 3 parallel reviewers → single revision → consolidated re-review → lesson writer. Per smoke-test protocol, only M1 produced this run; commit and exit.

produced.title: Chemistry of Life
produced.cb_unit: Unit 1 — Chemistry of Life (~8-11% of AP exam)
custom_slides: 12_carbs_lipids (carb polysaccharide table + lipid kinked-tail diagram + phospholipid bilayer sketch)

round1.A_peer_phd_biology: minor (4 minor issues — phosphate notation, triple bonds, room-temp qualifier, bond angle nuance)
round1.B_adversarial_top_student: minor (13 issues, 2 individually flagged critical-worthy but overall verdict minor — functional groups dump, cellulose/starch missing structural why)
round1.C_citation_checker: minor (1 issue — protein functions list not in initial reference)

review_verdict.reference_doc_expanded: references/ap-biology-m1-ref.md was extended to add: orbitals/by-mass language, H-bond donor/acceptor mechanic, versatile (not universal) solvent + hydrophobic effect, functional-groups property table, alpha-1,4 vs beta-1,4 glycosidic linkages, kinks-prevent-packing logic, amphipathic phospholipid, hydrophobic effect for tertiary structure, denaturation preserves 1°, phosphodiester bonds + antiparallel + 5'-3' directionality, protein functions per OpenStax 3.4
revisions_applied_round2: 04: replaced 'orbiting in shells' with 'electrons occupy orbitals in energy levels/shells'; added '96% by mass' qualifier
05: added H-bond donor/acceptor mechanic distinguishing from generic dipole-dipole
06: 'universal' → 'versatile/excellent for polar and ionic'; added hydrophobic-effect note
08: dropped triple bonds for carbon; phosphate now –OPO₃²⁻; functional groups paired with property/charge + downstream macromolecule
09: clarified lipids NOT polymers but DO use dehydration synthesis (3 H₂O released per triglyceride)
10/11: added second prompt on ribosome (dehydration synthesis) for symmetry
12: added α-1,4 vs β-1,4 explanation; led with kinks-prevent-packing logic; labeled phospholipid head/tails
13: hydrophobic interactions = exclusion effect not bond; denaturation preserves peptide bonds (1°); protein functions cited
14: added phosphodiester bonds, 5'-3' directionality, antiparallel strands, backbone-vs-base orientation
15: recap mirrors lesson — functional groups + protein 4 levels bullets added

round2.consolidated_reviewer: pass — all 14 round-1 items addressed; ~30 specific claims trace to expanded reference; 0 unsupported claims

course_completion_status.ap_biology: Smoke test complete (1/16 modules). Awaiting Mac launchd for TTS + ffmpeg + YouTube upload; awaiting next scheduled run for M2-M16 in normal mode.

anomalies: Initial reference snapshot (references/ap-biology-m1-ref.md) was intentionally minimal at start. Round-1 reviewers correctly flagged several AP-canonical concepts (orbitals not orbits, hydrophobic effect, antiparallel DNA, phosphodiester bonds, functional-group properties, α/β-glycosidic linkages, protein functions) that weren't in the initial snapshot. Reference was expanded mid-run to cover these, then outline revised. Final outline ships with 0 unsupported claims.

slide_kit.compare() supports only 2 columns; section 05 (covalent / ionic / hydrogen bond comparison) required 3 columns, so used deck.custom() instead. Acceptable workaround documented in produced.custom_slides.

Section 12 became dense (carbs + lipids in one slide). Reviewer noted concern; lesson writer kept section count at 16 per smoke-test SOP constraint. Future scaling: consider splitting 12a (carbs) and 12b (lipids) in normal mode.

The newly produced AP Biology M1 assets, references/ap-biology-m1-ref.md, _audit/ap-biology/*, and ROADMAP.md edit will be picked up by tomorrow's 9am Mac run automatically.
